Invalid capacitance should be rejected by the existing RLC validation.
Lower R should reduce damping and increase overshoot for the chosen cases.
Time, voltage, and current arrays should align for each simulation.
Sweep summaries should expose the expected educational metrics.
Response type should match standard damping ratio categories.
Damped natural frequency is only defined for underdamped response.
For zeta = 0.3, theoretical overshoot should be about 37.2%.
Critical damping should have no overshoot.
Peak time should be pi divided by damped natural frequency.
Critical damping does not have an underdamped peak time.
The simulated output should start at y0.
The simulated output velocity should start at v0.
For a unit step and enough time, final output should approach 1.
For a stable unit-step response, final velocity should approach zero.
An underdamped second-order step response should overshoot.
Number of samples must be positive.
The final simulation time must be greater than the initial time.
Step input should return the same value for any time.

RLC helper should return expected matrices.
DC motor helper should return expected matrices.
Simulation should return time, state, and output arrays with expected shapes.
The first state sample should equal x0.
Incompatible state-space matrix dimensions should raise ValueError.
Final time must be greater than initial time.
Number of time samples must be positive.
Time and response arrays must have the same length.
Empty arrays do not contain enough information for metrics.
A first-order response should have 10% to 90% rise time tau*log(9).
A first-order response should settle near -tau*log(0.02).
A monotonic first-order response should have no overshoot.
A response peaking at 1.2 with steady state 1.0 has 20% overshoot.
If the tail keeps leaving the settling band, settling time is None.
Numerator and denominator should be stored as float arrays.
A transfer function must have numerator coefficients.
A transfer function must have denominator coefficients.
The highest-order denominator coefficient must not be zero.
Second-order helper should return standard low-pass coefficients.
RLC helper should return capacitor-voltage transfer coefficients.
Step response simulation should return matching time and output arrays.
A unit-gain first-order step response should approach 1.
Final simulation time must be greater than initial time.
Number of generated time samples must be positive.
Explicit time samples must be one-dimensional.
Explicit time samples must be strictly increasing.

Mean and covariance weights should match the sigma point count.
Predict should preserve the state vector shape.

A direct measurement should pull the estimate toward the measurement.

A scalar UKF should smooth noisy direct measurements.
The CFL number should be c*dt/dx.
Parameters with CFL <= 1 should pass the stability check.
Parameters with CFL > 1 should fail when not raising.
The helper should raise for unstable parameters when configured.

The sine helper should return one value per x point.
The triangular helper should return one value per x point.
The zero-velocity helper should match x shape and contain zeros.
The solver should return the documented result dictionary fields.
The spatial and time grids should be one-dimensional arrays.
Fixed-displacement boundaries should remain fixed for all time.
Free boundaries should copy their nearest interior neighbors.
The simulator should reject unstable explicit time steps by default.

The grid needs at least two boundaries and one interior point.
Provided time step must be positive.
Only supported boundary condition types should be accepted.
A stable fixed-end sine mode should remain bounded.
The stable solver should produce finite displacement values.
A default displacement pulse should propagate, not remain static.
Positive initial velocity should move the first interior step upward.
With zero curvature, the first wave step should advance by v0*dt.
